hooks.prompt: Valleys at Ashebrook docs freshness check. This check is DETECTION ONLY: you (the hook agent) must not edit, create, or delete any file and must not run git write commands. This no-edit rule governs your OWN actions only — it does NOT govern the working tree you observe. Uncommitted changes already present in the working tree (for example, documentation the session just updated via the docs-updater subagent) are EXPECTED and are NOT a violation to report. Your only job is to detect whether changed SOURCE code lacks a matching DOCUMENTATION update (drift). Step 1: run exactly `git status --porcelain` as a single command (it is pre-approved; do not add pipes, redirects, `||`, or chain it with anything — compound commands are not covered by the permission rules and will be denied). If you need content detail, `git diff HEAD` and `git diff HEAD --name-only` are also pre-approved as standalone commands. Step 2: identify changed or untracked SOURCE/CONFIG files — anything under src/, scripts/, or test/, or any of wrangler.toml, astro.config.mjs, package.json. If there are NONE (for example only documentation changed — *.md files, docs/**, README, CHANGELOG, ROADMAP, or ADRs), ALLOW THE STOP: respond that no source files changed and there is no drift to check. Do NOT treat uncommitted doc edits as a problem. Step 3: otherwise use the Read, Grep, and Glob tools (no other shell commands) to spot-check whether the docs owned by .claude/agents/docs-updater.md — AGENTS.md (endpoints, data model, commands, bindings), README.md, SETUP.md, SECURITY.md, and CHANGELOG.md — still match the changed code. Check only sections relevant to the changed source files, not a full audit. Step 4: if the docs are consistent — including the case where they were already updated in this session's working tree to match the changed source — ALLOW THE STOP. Only block the stop when changed SOURCE files have no corresponding doc update: block with a reason that lists the changed source files, the specific stale doc sections, and this instruction: 'Invoke the docs-updater subagent to fix exactly this drift, then finish.'
